summary: A deterministic, fail-closed clean-root queue/package history reconstruction path is implemented and fixture-verified. The item remains open because this evidence does not substitute for the required real Tier-A clean-root restore, semantic recipe replay, package/lineage proof, or completed seven-day soak prerequisite.

apply_guards: source database is current schema, integrity-clean, foreign-key-clean, and drained
path registry is readable JSON and hash-bound
manifest set and nested scene/package payloads are SHA-256 sealed
target database and registry view must be new paths
scheduler events replay from pending state in valid lease/terminal order
replayed job states and attempts must equal the restored source state
scene, job, package, and event tables are compared before publication
target integrity and foreign keys pass and WAL journal mode is preserved
registry view binds source, manifest, path-registry, target, and event-chain hashes

validation.ruff_command: python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/daz/__init__.py src/maskfactory/daz/reconstruction.py src/maskfactory/cli.py tests/test_daz_reconstruction.py
validation.format_command: python -m ruff format --check src/maskfactory/daz/__init__.py src/maskfactory/daz/reconstruction.py src/maskfactory/cli.py tests/test_daz_reconstruction.py

remaining_gates: complete D11-07 seven-day soak with daily restart
restore current Tier-A backup into a real empty root
rebuild the live asset-registry projection from portable authority
verify one frozen recipe semantic replay and one Tier-B scene/package
verify mapping and downstream lineage hashes on the restored root
authority_boundary: Fixture-backed code and tests prove the reconstruction mechanism only. They are not clean-root production restore evidence and do not complete MF-P9-12.08.
